user.title: LADWP Energy Cost Calculator
user.description: Pick the cumulative energy sensors (Wh/kWh/MWh) that meter your home's electricity. Solar and load are optional and add savings/usage breakdowns. Power (W/kW) sensors are not supported; if you only have power, feed it through a Riemann-sum or utility-meter helper first.

data.name: Name
data.grid_energy_entity: Grid energy meter — net Wh/kWh to/from LADWP (required)
data.grid_invert_sign: My grid meter counts down when importing

data_description.grid_energy_entity: A bidirectional energy meter. It rises when importing from LADWP and falls when exporting excess solar. On an Enphase/CT-clamp setup this is the grid (net) energy value, not panel output.
data_description.solar_energy_entity: Your cumulative solar production energy (kWh, only ever rising). Used only to estimate solar savings; it does not change the bill. On Enphase this is the lifetime production energy sensor.
data_description.load_energy_entity: Your cumulative whole-home consumption energy (kWh, only ever rising; load = solar + grid import). Used only for the consumption breakdown; it does not change the bill.
data_description.billing_period: Monthly or bi-monthly, matching your LADWP plan (Bi-monthly cycles span two months).
data_description.last_bill_date: The start date of your current bill (the day the meter was last read).
data_description.grid_invert_sign: Leave off if your grid energy counter rises when importing from LADWP. Turn on only if it is reversed (rises when exporting), which would otherwise swap delivered and received.

error.grid_energy_required: A grid meter entity is required — this is the sensor that measures net flow between your home and LADWP.

init.title: LADWP Energy Cost Options
init.description: Update your LADWP Energy Cost Calculator configuration

data.grid_invert_sign: My grid meter counts down when importing

data_description.last_bill_date: The start date of your current bill. Cycles are anchored here and step forward by your billing period.
data_description.grid_invert_sign: Leave off if your grid energy counter rises when importing from LADWP. Turn on only if it is reversed (rises when exporting).

rate_fetch_failed.title: Could not update LADWP rates
rate_fetch_failed.description:
Home Assistant could not fetch the latest electricity rates from ladwp.com ({url}).

Reason: {detail}.

The integration keeps using the most recent rates it successfully fetched and will retry automatically — usually no action is needed. If this persists, check Home Assistant's internet access, or update rates manually by running `scripts/fetch_ladwp_rates.py build`. This message clears itself once a fetch succeeds.
